17.title: Declaring the name
17.text: Jesus closes the prayer saying he has declared the Father's name and will declare it still. Throughout the prayer, the theme of the 'name' and of knowing God stands as the very heart of life eternal.

18.title: The brook Cedron
18.text: Jesus crosses the brook Cedron, a valley east of Jerusalem, to a garden (Gethsemane). The path leads toward the Mount of Olives, the setting of his agony and arrest.

18.template: cometh thither with lanterns and torches and {0}.

They dropped their weapons and prayed

18.question: According to verse 14, what counsel had Caiaphas given the Jews?
options: That one man should die for the people
That tribute should be paid to Caesar
That the temple should be rebuilt

the servants and officers stood there, who had made a fire of {0}; for it was cold

18.title: I taught openly
18.text: Before the high priest, Jesus notes that he taught in the synagogue and the temple, whither the Jews always resort, and said nothing in secret. His ministry was public, not a hidden plot.

18.template: If I have spoken evil, bear witness of the {0}: but if well, why smitest thou me?

19.title: The scourging before the cross
19.text: The chapter opens with Pilate scourging Jesus. Roman scourging was a brutal flogging that often preceded crucifixion, and here it marks the beginning of the suffering John narrates with restraint.

19.question: How did the soldiers mock Jesus as they hailed him?

19.template: And Pilate saith unto them, Behold the {0}!
by our law he ought to die, because he made himself the {0} of God.

19.question: What did Jesus answer Pilate about his power?
options: Thou couldest have no power at all against me, except it were given thee from above
Thou hast no power over the Jews
Thy power cometh only from Cæsar
It is the law that gave thee power over me

19.template: in a place that is called the Pavement, but in the Hebrew, {0}.

19.question: What did the chief priests answer when Pilate asked, “Shall I crucify your King?”
options: We have no king but Cæsar
We have no law but Cæsar

19.template: into a place called the place of a skull, which is called in the Hebrew {0}:
And the writing was, Jesus of {0} the King of the Jews.

pairs.right: One of the three languages the title was written in
Was nigh to the place where Jesus was crucified
What Pilate wrote and put on the cross

19.title: The seamless coat
19.text: Jesus' coat was “without seam, woven from the top throughout.” Rather than tear it, the soldiers cast lots for it, unknowingly fulfilling the prophecy of Psalm 22 about parting his garments.

19.question: Why did the soldiers decide not to rend Jesus' coat?
options: So they could cast lots for it, whose it should be
Because it was to be returned to Jesus' mother
Because a priest had claimed it

19.question: What did Jesus say to his mother as she stood by the cross with the disciple he loved?

options: A bone of him shall not be broken
They shall look on him whom they pierced

We have no king but Cæsar

The place where the new sepulchre lay
Wherein was never man yet laid

20.title: The first day of the week
20.text: John records that Mary Magdalene came to the sepulchre early, while it was yet dark, on the first day of the week. This detail marks the morning of the resurrection — the reason Christians came to gather on Sundays.

20.template: The first day of the week cometh Mary Magdalene early, when it was yet dark, unto the {0}, and seeth the stone taken away from the sepulchre.

20.template: And the napkin, that was about his {0}, not lying with the linen clothes, but wrapped together in a place by itself.

20.question: By what other name was Thomas, one of the twelve, called?

20.title: My Lord and my God
20.text: Thomas' confession, “My Lord and my God,” is the most direct declaration of Jesus' divinity in all of John's gospel, closing the circle opened in the very first verse: “the Word was God.”

21.title: An epilogue by the sea
21.text: John 21 reads like an epilogue: after the resurrection climax of chapter 20, the gospel returns to Galilee and the sea of Tiberias — where the disciples first followed — to restore Peter and seal the beloved disciple's testimony.

21.question: Which disciple is called “Didymus” when gathered with the others by the sea?
